title: Reasons for determination — July 3, 2026
summary: Canada's trade tribunal ruled that Chinese steel pipe imports unfairly undercut domestic prices, so Canadian steel pipe makers can now operate without competing against artificially cheap imports.

title: Reasons for determination — June 24, 2026
summary: Canadian importers of hot-rolled steel sheet now face provisional duties up to 25% after the trade tribunal found domestic producers were being harmed by below-cost foreign sales.

title: Determination — June 18, 2026
summary: Canada's trade tribunal ruled that imports of certain steel products no longer injure domestic producers, so tariffs on these items will be removed, lowering costs for Canadian manufacturers who use steel.

title: Determination — June 9, 2026
summary: Canadian importers of certain steel products will now face higher tariffs after the Canadian International Trade Tribunal ruled that dumped imports are injuring domestic steel makers.

title: Reasons for determination — June 1, 2026
summary: The Canadian International Trade Tribunal has issued a new determination on June 1, 2026, but the specific ruling details aren't provided in your source material, so I cannot write an accurate summary of what changed or why it matters for Canadian businesses.

title: Public hearing — May 25, 2026
summary: The Canadian International Trade Tribunal is holding a public hearing on May 25, 2026, where Canadian businesses can present evidence and arguments about a trade matter under investigation.

title: Reasons for finding — May 19, 2026
summary: Canadian manufacturers can now import certain steel products without facing anti-dumping duties after the CITT determined foreign competitors are no longer selling below cost.

title: Determination — May 15, 2026
summary: Canadian manufacturers of wooden bedroom furniture can now export to the U.S. without facing anti-dumping duties after the tariff was removed following a review.
